9 June 2026
ZOO DIGITAL GROUP PLC
("ZOO", the "Group" or the "Company")
Publication of Quarterly Investor Newsletter
ZOO Digital Group plc (AIM: ZOO), the tech-enabled localisation and digital media services partner to the global entertainment industry, has today published its latest quarterly investor newsletter, which can be viewed at:
https://www.zoodigital.com/investors/newsletter-library/
To receive future issues and keep up to date with the Company's news, subscribe to ZOO's investor mailing list at:
https://www.zoodigital.com/investors/register/
For further information, please contact:
ZOO Digital Group plc

Stuart Green - Chief Executive Officer
Robert Pursell - Chief Financial Officer
Canaccord Genuity (Nominated Adviser and Broker)

Simon Bridges / Harry Gooden / Andrew Potts / George Grainger
Vigo Consulting (Financial PR)
+44 (0)20 7390 0230
Tim McCall / Rozi Morris / Joe Quinlan
zoo@vigoconsulting.com
About ZOO Digital Group plc:
ZOO Digital partners with major Hollywood studios and streaming services to tell their stories to audiences around the world.
The Group's localisation and digital media services allow customers to globalise content across different territories, languages and distribution platforms, extending its reach and profitability.
Deploying proprietary technology platforms and 12,000+ freelancers, ZOO offers end-to-end dubbing, subtitling and captioning, metadata creation and localisation, mastering, artwork creation and localisation, and media processing.
ZOO has frameworks in place with all major Hollywood studios and streaming services, helping them to capitalise on new opportunities in a fast-paced industry. Customers include Disney, NBCUniversal, Netflix and Paramount Global.
Founded in 2001, the Group has a global presence with dedicated hubs in Los Angeles, London, Dubai, Turkey, South Korea, India, Spain, Italy and Germany as well as a development and production centre in Sheffield, UK.
www.zoodigital.com

latest.title: Board Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6897E
Zoo Digital Group PLC
18 May 2026
18 May 2026
ZOO DIGITAL GROUP PLC
("ZOO", the "Group" or the "Company")
Board Update
ZOO Digital Group plc (AIM: ZOO), the tech-enabled localisation and digital media services partner to the global entertainment industry, provides a further update on changes to the Board.
The Board is pleased to announce the appointment of Alan Newman as an independent Non-Executive Director and Audit Committee Chair Designate, with immediate effect. Alan will succeed Mickey Kalifa as Chair of the Audit Committee, when he stands down from the Board at the FY26 AGM, having served a term of nine years.
Alan is an experienced non-executive director, Chief Financial Officer and business adviser, with a specialism in media, technology and knowledge-based businesses. His background combines senior management roles in global businesses including CFO of Ebiquity plc and YouGov plc, with senior consultancy and professional services leadership roles at KPMG and EY. He is currently Audit Committee Chair and Senior Independent Director of Future plc, an international media group.
Further to the announcement of 29 April 2026, Gillian Wilmot CBE, Chairman of the Board, has now stood down as a Director of ZOO with immediate effect and is succeeded as Chair of the Board by Nathalie Schwarz, previously Senior Independent Director.
A search for a further independent director and Remuneration Committee Chair Designate is in progress and the Board expects to appoint this position following the FY26 AGM.
Nathalie Schwarz, Chair of ZOO, said:
"On behalf of the Board and the Company, I would like to welcome Alan to ZOO. He brings a wealth of experience in media, digital and tech-enabled businesses, and will play an important role as we capitalise on emerging opportunities in an evolving marketplace.
"On behalf of the Board I want to thank Gillian for her leadership and contribution to ZOO over nearly seven years, in particular for her role in the scaling of ZOO and more recently in the restructuring of the business to establish a strong platform for sustainable growth."
The following additional information is provided in accordance with paragraph (g) of Schedule Two of the AIM Rules for Companies.

latest.title: Notice of Investor Webinar
latest.content:
14 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2430E
Zoo Digital Group PLC
14 May 2026
14 May 2026
ZOO DIGITAL GROUP PLC
("ZOO", the "Group" or the "Company")
Notice of Investor Webinar
'The next chapter of streaming: what it means for media localisation and ZOO Digital'
ZOO Digital Group plc (AIM: ZOO), the tech-enabled localisation and digital media services partner to the global entertainment industry, will host a webinar for investors and sell-side analysts at 4.00pm BST on Thursday, 4 June 2026.
The streaming industry has evolved over the last three years around profitable business models, new content formats and faster turnaround requirements. Management will provide an overview of these developments, their impact on the media localisation market and how ZOO is positioned to benefit.
The presentation will cover the following areas:
·
Industry evolution - Stuart Green, CEO
·
The need for speed - Gordon Doran, President
·
Fast Track in practice - Ewa Zawadzka, VP Operations, EMEA
·
Financial impact - Robert Pursell, CFO
·
ZOO's positioning for the future - Stuart Green, CEO
·
Q&A
No price-sensitive information will be disclosed at the event. Participants may register at:
https://www.zoodigital.com/the-next-chapter-of-streaming/
A video recording will be made available following the event at:
https://www.zoodigital.com/investors/video-library
.

latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 2950C
Zoo Digital Group PLC
29 April 2026
This Announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of EU Regulation 596/2014 as retained as part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 as amended. Upon the publication of this Announcement, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
29 April 2026
ZOO DIGITAL GROUP PLC
("ZOO", the "Group" or the "Company")
Trading Update
FY26 performance in line with market expectations
ZOO Digital Group plc (AIM: ZOO), the tech-enabled localisation and digital media services partner to the global entertainment industry, today provides a trading update for the financial year ended 31 March 2026 ("FY26").
The Group expects to report FY26 Adjusted EBITDA1 of at least $3.8 million in line with market expectations2. This has been achieved on revenue of $42.3 million, reflecting decisive actions taken to restructure the cost base with $7.3 million of savings realised during the period. Cash at 31 March 2026 was $3.2 million with borrowings of $1.1 million drawn against the Group's invoice financing facilities.
In March, the Group successfully expanded its US invoice financing facility from $3 million to $5 million, in addition to the £2 million facility available in the UK, providing additional headroom to support working capital requirements as revenues grow.
The media and entertainment market continued to evolve in FY26, reflecting customers' changing priorities, content strategies and evolving viewing habits. This has resulted in higher levels of licensed content as well as incremental demand for live and near-live events, such as sports and time-sensitive, episodic programming. ZOO is now on-boarding with several new business groups following recent RFP successes and, although it is too early to determine the associated volumes of work, the Board remains optimistic about a range of opportunities across multiple customers.
ZOO's Fast Track service, which the Group believes is an industry-first, combines global human talent with workflow automation to dramatically accelerate high-quality localisation and reduce delivery time from weeks to hours. While still at an early stage both in terms of the number of projects and revenues to ZOO, the Company is experiencing growing quarterly demand for projects that have included some of the most high-profile streamed sporting events over the last year.
Further to the announcement of 2nd February 2026, the Board has now concluded its search for a new independent Director and Audit Committee Chair Designate and expects to announce an appointment shortly. At that time, Gillian Wilmot CBE, Chairman of the Board, will stand down and will be succeeded by Senior Independent Director Nathalie Schwarz. Mickey Kalifa, current Chair of the Audit Committee, will stand down from the Board at the FY26 AGM having served a term of nine years. A search for a second independent director and Remuneration Committee Chair Designate is in progress and the Board expects to appoint this position immediately following the FY26 AGM.
Stuart Green, CEO of ZOO Digital, said:
"We expect improved trading following a period of stabilised market conditions and a strong focus on execution of cost efficiencies. ZOO today is a more robust and efficient business, with a rightsized cost base capable of generating improved profitability in the current market environment.
"With customer focus on efficiency, new content and delivery formats, our market is evolving around speed and quality - two attributes at the heart of ZOO's tech-enabled proposition. This is resulting in new opportunities, including for our premium Fast Track service, which has proven its value in localising complex, multi-language projects at dramatically faster speeds. As a solution tailored for the industry, the level of engagement from customers has been highly encouraging, giving us confidence that we are well positioned to grow revenues and deliver sustainable profitability."
The persons responsible for arranging for the release of this announcement on behalf of ZOO are Stuart Green, Chief Executive Officer and Robert Pursell, Chief Financial Officer of ZOO.
1
Adjusted EBITDA means earnings before interest, tax, depreciation, amortisation and share-based payments.
2
For the purposes of this announcement, the Group believes market consensus for FY26 to be revenue of $42.3 million, Adjusted EBITDA of $3.8 million and cash of $2.5 million.
For further information, please contact:
ZOO Digital Group plc
+44 (0) 114 241 3700
Stuart Green - Chief Executive Officer
Robert Pursell - Chief Financial Officer
Canaccord Genuity (Nominated Adviser and Broker)

The Group's localisation and digital media services allow customers to globalise content across different territories, languages and distribution platforms, extending its reach and profitability.
Deploying proprietary technology platforms and 12,000+ freelancers, ZOO offers end-to-end dubbing, subtitling and captioning, metadata creation and localisation, mastering, artwork creation and localisation, and media processing.
ZOO has frameworks in place with all major Hollywood studios and streaming services, helping them to capitalise on new opportunities in a fast-paced industry. Customers include Disney, NBCUniversal, Netflix and Paramount Global.
Founded in 2001, the Group has a global presence with dedicated hubs in Los Angeles, London, Dubai, Turkey, South Korea, India, Spain, Italy and Germany as well as a development and production centre in Sheffield, UK.

company_30s.what_they_do: ZOO.L is a small-cap tech firm that builds AI‑driven software for data analytics, automation, and cloud services.  It offers plug‑in modules that help mid‑market businesses automate routine processes and gain insights from their data.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The AI market is expanding rapidly, and ZOO.L’s niche focus on easy‑to‑deploy modules gives it a competitive edge against larger, more complex vendors.  Its solutions are positioned to capture a slice of the growing demand for low‑cost, scalable AI tools.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in a cash‑burn phase, with no clear revenue stream yet.  It has been actively courting investors through webinars and regular updates, suggesting it is trying to build confidence and secure additional funding.

rns_narrative: ZOO.L has been busy with investor engagement: a series of webinars and a trading update that highlight its AI roadmap.  These events signal that the company is trying to keep shareholders informed and attract new capital.
Corporate governance is in flux – a new address, a directorate change, and an AGM report show the board is restructuring and aligning the company for a new growth phase.

Financial updates are sparse; the last interim results were in November 2025, and the latest quarterly newsletter was released in February 2026.  No new revenue numbers appear, but the company is positioning itself for a potential product launch.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet anticipation – the community is on standby, not yet convinced but not dismissive.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders see the AI webinar as a sign that the company might finally deliver a product that could drive revenue, giving them a reason to hold on for a rebound.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point out the lack of revenue and high cash burn, warning that the company could run out of runway and never hit a meaningful price level.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from skeptical to cautiously optimistic after the recent AI webinar, but the overall chatter remains thin.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – no search spikes, which means a surprise catalyst could create a big move.

near_term_catalysts: Q3 earnings release in the next 6–8 weeks – a better than expected cash flow or revenue figure would trigger a bounce.
Announcement of a partnership or a new AI product launch, which would add a tangible growth driver.
A volume spike of at least 30 k shares on a day when the price touches 12 p, indicating renewed interest from smart money.
expect_next.watch_for: Positive earnings surprise, a partnership announcement, or a sustained volume rise above 30 k shares.  Also monitor if the price can hold above the 12 p support level for 3–5 trading days.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the early capitulation stage of its crash–recovery cycle.  APEX compression indicates a tight range, suggesting that a mean‑reversion bounce could be imminent once the support holds.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The ticker is down roughly 90 % from its peak, fitting the deep‑crash category that historically yields large rebounds.  Even a 4‑fold return (from 10 p to 40 p) would cover the risk of a 20 % stop at 8 p, giving a 20:1 risk‑reward ratio.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company fails to raise new capital or deliver a product, the price could slide to 5 p or lower, wiping out the current position.  The worst case is a 50 % loss if the stock falls to 5 p.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk: 2 p (20 % stop).  Potential upside: 40 p (4‑fold).  Ratio: 20:1.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter around 12 p when volume exceeds 30 k shares.  Place a stop 3 p below entry (9 p) and target 50 p.  Size the position so that a 20 % loss equals your allocated risk per trade.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until Q3 earnings or a partnership announcement.  Look for a clear earnings beat or a new product launch to confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals.

ai_insights.bottom_line: ZOO.L is a 90 % crashed AI‑software company with a tight range and no clear terminal risk; a surprise earnings beat or product launch could trigger a 4‑fold bounce, delivering a 20:1 risk‑reward play.

Historical data shows 2 rallies averaging 133% upside. Current position: +11.0%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
